message:
fix(abi): harden 17 ABI modules with iconv state machine, malloc arena guards, math domain checks, and resolv DNS wire validation

Comprehensive ABI hardening pass across the frankenlibc surface:

iconv: Adds a full state-machine based converter with shift-state tracking,
output buffer overflow protection, and E2BIG/EILSEQ/EINVAL errno parity with
glibc. New test suite covers round-trip encoding, partial output buffering,
and invalid sequence rejection.

malloc: Adds arena-based allocation guards with alignment validation,
memalign/posix_memalign power-of-two enforcement, pvalloc page rounding, and
calloc overflow detection (SIZE_MAX/nmemb). New tests verify all allocation
variants including edge cases.

math: Adds domain/range checks for lgamma, tgamma, j0/j1/jn Bessel functions,
and y0/y1/yn with proper errno EDOM/ERANGE propagation. Fixes edge cases
where Inf/NaN inputs produced incorrect results without setting errno.

resolv: Hardens DNS wire format parsing with label compression loop detection,
maximum label length enforcement (63 bytes), total name length validation (253
chars), and proper NXDOMAIN/SERVFAIL error code propagation.

Also touches: dirent (readdir_r errno), errno (thread-local accessor), fortify
(__chk bounds), glibc_internal (sgetspent_r cast cleanup), locale (setlocale
null handling), process (waitpid status macros), pthread (barrier/rwlock edge
cases), rpc (XDR bounds), startup (auxv parsing), stdlib (strtol base
validation), string (strsignal thread safety), unistd (sysconf/pathconf
additions).

- 17 ABI source files hardened (+489 -76 lines)
- 4 test files with new conformance coverage
- 1 resolv core module fix
- .beads/issues.jsonl: track hardening tasks

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
